subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **120 wooden stone blocks** (30 per player in 4 colors)
- **5 site boards** (Market, Pyramids, Temple, Burial Chamber, Obelisks) — double-sided (A/B)
- **8 boat tiles** (various sizes: 1-4 spaces)
- **Market cards**
- **Round cards**
- **1 scoring track**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place 5 site boards** — Use A sides for first game.
2. **Shuffle and place boats** — Available boats change each round.
3. **Shuffle market cards** per round requirements.
4. **Shuffle round cards** — 6 rounds total.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **30 stone blocks** in your color.
- **5 stones** on your personal supply sled.
- **Remaining 25** in your quarry (general supply).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Imhotep is a strategy game of loading stones onto boats and sailing them to building sites. The twist: anyone can sail any boat, so your stones might not end up where you planned.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE of:
1. **Get new stones** — Take 3 stones from your quarry to your sled.
2. **Load 1 stone** — Place 1 stone from your sled onto a boat.
3. **Sail a boat** — Move a boat (with minimum load) to a site.
4. **Play a market card** — Use a previously gained blue market card.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Loading Boats
- Place your stone on the next empty space on any boat.
- Boats hold 1-4 stones depending on size.

#### Sailing Boats
- Choose a boat that has met its minimum load requirement.
- Sail it to any unoccupied site this round.
- Stones are unloaded in order (front to back) and placed at the site.

#### Building Sites
- **Market** — Take market cards (blue/green/purple with various effects).
- **Pyramids** — Build layers of a pyramid; score based on visible stones.
- **Temple** — Place stones on temple rows; score for top visible stones.
- **Burial Chamber** — Place stones in a grid; score for connected groups.
- **Obelisks** — Stack stones to build tall obelisks; tallest scores most.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Boat minimum** — Each boat has a minimum load before it can sail.
- **One boat per site** — Only 1 boat can dock at each site per round.
- **Unload order** — First stone loaded is first placed at the site (important for scoring).
- **B-side sites** — Alternate scoring rules for experienced players.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### During Game
- Temple scores at end of each round (top visible stones).

#### After 6 Rounds
- **Pyramids** — Points based on stone visibility in the pyramid layers.
- **Temple** — Final round scoring.
- **Burial Chamber** — Points for connected groups of your stones.
- **Obelisks** — Most stones in each obelisk scores most.
- **Market cards** — Green cards scored.
- **Highest total wins.**

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Load order matters** — First stone on a boat gets placed first at the site.
- **Sail opponents' boats** — Send their stones to less favorable sites.
- **Balance sites** — Don't neglect any site completely.
- **Market cards** — Blue cards provide extra actions and flexibility.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Boat manipulation** — Loading last and sailing first gives you the most control.
- **Denial sailing** — Sail a boat loaded with opponent stones to a bad site.
- **Obelisk commitment** — Once you start an obelisk, you need to keep building it.
- **Burial chamber planning** — Connected groups are exponentially more valuable.
